figures.caption: Figure 6: System architecture of JARVIS, illustrated with an origami boat-folding task, comprising four components: (1) Pre-Task Planner generating a structured JSON task plan from user voice input and retrieved images via Gemini Flash and SAM3, (2) In-Task Planner verifying task state and either advancing with audio confirmation or invoking sub-planning upon failure, (3) Visual Renderer localizing targets via Gemini Pro and projecting guidance into 3D space using Meta Quest depth data, and (4) User Interaction supporting in-situ voice clarification with real-time AR updates.

Figure 10: Step-level completion heatmap across three systems (JARVIS, arrow baseline, image baseline) and four task types (R2R, R2V, V2R, V2V). Each row represents a participant and each column an action step, color-coded by outcome: correct completion, step omission, technical error, incorrect order, and skip guidance.

Figure 7: JARVIS guidance examples across the four user study tasks: (1) coffee machine latte-making with target configuration preview, gesture, motion, tool, and bounding box, (2) digital painting with tool and motion trajectory overlays, (3) origami boat folding with gesture, shape preview and arrow guidance, (4) gaming task with state cues, tool, bounding box and arrow indicators.
Figure 2: Examples from the formative study comparing video, text, and image guidance across representative tasks.
Figure 3: Comparison of error rate, completion time, and user rating across three guidance modalities: Text (T), Image (I), and Video (V). Significant differences are indicated by brackets.
Figure 4: Cross-Reality Step Types. Steps are categorized into four types based on whether the instructional referent and intended action occur in the real or virtual domain.
